incidents.vector: Custom GenieLocker ransomware and modified LockBit 3 / Babuk variants deployed against Russian enterprises, with over 70 attacks in the past year.
incidents.impact: Ransom demands have escalated to hundreds of thousands of dollars. Approximately one in five victims pays. The group collaborates with other pro-Ukrainian hacking collectives.

incidents.vector: Three parallel attack clusters used USB-propagated malware, espionage toolkits with multiple backdoors and RATs, and novel loaders to establish persistent access across government endpoints.
incidents.impact: Multiple government endpoints compromised by three distinct China-aligned threat groups operating simultaneously. Persistent access was established using at least six different malware families.

incidents.vector: Ransomware deployed against cargo management servers at Spain's Port of Vigo; initial access method under investigation.
incidents.impact: Digital systems disconnected from external networks. Cargo operations forced to manual procedures and paper documentation. No timeline for restoration; port will not reconnect until full security is guaranteed.

incidents.vector: Compromised versions of the LiteLLM Python package (v1.82.7 and v1.82.8) were published to PyPI, likely via a hijacked maintainer account, with malware that exfiltrates credentials every 50 minutes.
incidents.impact: Package has 3 million daily downloads and is present in roughly 36% of cloud environments. Malware extracted cloud credentials, API keys and cryptocurrency wallets. Full downstream impact still being assessed.

incidents.vector: AI-generated phishing lures delivered via Railway Platform as a Service, exploiting OAuth tokens to compromise Microsoft accounts.
incidents.impact: 344 confirmed victim organizations across construction, law firms and healthcare. Attackers gained persistent access to Microsoft accounts and organizational data via stolen OAuth tokens.

incidents.victim: 3 million+ compromised IoT devices / multiple DDoS targets
incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) built from compromised routers and web cameras launched hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks with extortion demands.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 DDoS attack commands from Aisuru alone; victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. DOJ, FBI and DoD jointly seized domains and infrastructure, dismantling the botnets.

incidents.vector: Self-propagating CanisterWorm spreads via exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters and Redis servers; deploys a wiper payload when victim timezone and locale match Iran.
incidents.impact: Destroys all data on Kubernetes clusters and local machines matching Iranian locale. Operated from tamperproof blockchain-based command infrastructure resistant to takedowns.

incidents.vector: Injected credential-stealing malware into official releases of the Trivy and KICS vulnerability scanners via compromised GitHub Actions workflows.
incidents.impact: SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens and cryptocurrency wallets stolen. Over 1,000 SaaS environments impacted. Mandiant responding; expected wave of follow-on extortion attacks.

incidents.victim: Organizations using Cisco firewalls and SD-WAN
incidents.vector: Active exploitation of multiple Cisco SD-WAN and firewall zero-day vulnerabilities including CVE-2026-20131; used by Interlock ransomware for initial access.
incidents.impact: CISA added CVE-2026-20131 to KEV catalog. Interlock ransomware operators are chaining zero-day firewall exploits with network intrusion for ransomware deployment.

incidents.vector: Code injection via unauthenticated API endpoint CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8); attackers built working exploits within hours of advisory publication without a public proof-of-concept.
incidents.impact: Remote code execution on vulnerable instances. API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic and AWS extracted, enabling lateral movement to connected databases and services. CISA added to KEV catalog.

incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS) — state-directed hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Compromised endpoint management systems (Microsoft Intune) to push data-wiping malware across 200,000+ endpoints, servers and mobile devices in 79 countries.
incidents.impact: Operations shut down globally; 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland alone. Employee devices wiped and defaced. CISA issued alert urging endpoint management hardening. Company communicating via WhatsApp.

incidents.victim: Individuals and enterprises in Middle East (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Iran)
incidents.vector: Thousands of conflict-themed domains registered for phishing, fake storefronts, cryptocurrency donation scams and credential harvesting, impersonating telcos, airlines and government entities.
incidents.impact: 7,381 phishing URLs identified across 1,881 hostnames since the start of the Iran conflict. Widespread credential theft, financial fraud and fake humanitarian donation scams targeting enterprise and consumer sectors.
